methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Darkhive is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Steve Turner that has raised $22M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers spanning both public (2) and private (8) companies operating in the same sector, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI at equivalent stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base value, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $44M. Peer-median analysis incorporates four same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $125M to $500M (Stark Defence at the high end, NODA AI at the low), generating a median of $225M. The disparity between methods reflects heterogeneity in Series A defense-tech valuations.

Triangulation across both methods produces a low estimate of $37M, median of $134M, and high of $259M. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty: peer valuations span a 4x spread, and Darkhive's positioning relative to high-growth outliers like Stark Defence ($500M) versus capital-efficient operators near $125M remains unresolved by available data. Key uncertainties include customer traction, technical differentiation, and whether the company's capital efficiency aligns with peers at the lower or upper quartile. A mid-range estimate of $130–$150M appears defensible, contingent on validation of growth metrics not captured here.
